experiment: The control caught us: the composition flip on Cabinet was +100k training, not the calibration loss (identical flip at calib=0, err even lower). Real discovery: Cab's jumpy null was an undertraining artifact — pre-registered 600k jum/van test queued (does the null flip?). Fleet: Ori n-boost wave -> Cab-600k -> M2 from-scratch calib runs (prediction committed, expectations low).
dev: Part 8 published (the autopsy + the surviving findings: disc_err_gap as a free convergence diagnostic; pyramid works on converged models). Next dev: harvest Ori n=8 -> anchor update; Cab-600k verdict; paper v3 folds the control story + possibly a flipped 3/3 anchor.
eta: Ori wave finals ~23:00-01:00; Cab-600k finals ~tomorrow morning; M2 drains after.

history.title: Part 1: From scratch to Phase 2 on HopperHop (world-model tutorial)
Part 2: The mechanism-check that saved a campaign (8 mirages)
Part 3: The latent was already the abstraction (value-sufficiency probe)
Part 4: The redundancy criterion — the paper (3/3 latent classes measured)
Part 5: Using abstraction right — aim at planning axes (iter-30 plan)
Part 6: Pre-registered predictions, published before results
Part 6 UPDATE: predictor scored — k2 4/4, k8 0/3, k=4 optimal
Part 7: The predictor transfers domains (weakly, as predicted)
Part 8: The control caught us — budget, not calibration; Cab null = undertraining
